tax: Ignis Elementalis Nobilis is placed at species rank beneath Ignis (Pyric order, Fire Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a three-layer combustion architecture (cool outer, white-hot middle, violet-ultraviolet core), ability to sustain form without consuming fuel, and three-ring dispersal sign. It is separated from its sibling Ignis elementalis major by its precise three-layer differentiated combustion architecture and fuel-independent persistence versus the major form's undifferentiated massive single-layer deep-red combustion; the two share the genus body-plan but never the the undifferentiated single-layer major body. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: a precisely differentiated flame humanoid with a cool blue outer layer, white-hot middle shell, and violet-ultraviolet inner core sustained by deep primary vent-pressure.
ap: Ignis Elementalis Nobilis stands 3–4 m — comparable to the nobilis Terrae form, smaller than the Ignis major. Its silhouette is a humanoid of visibly differentiated flame layers: a cool blue outer body that does not ignite contact, a white-hot middle shell visible as a bright ring through the outer layer, and a brilliant violet-ultraviolet core at the centre of the torso-mass. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: a cool outer flame layer that does not scorch on touch — the only Ignis form that can be approached to 0.5 m without contact burns, because the outer layer is below ignition temperature; a violet-ultraviolet inner core visible as a brilliant spot through two outer layers, distinguishable from the minor's blue-white by the concentric layering around it; and three concentric char-rings on dispersal: an outer cool-soot ring at 1 m, a white-hot char ring at 50 cm (8 cm deep), and a violet-soot central mark of 15 cm. It moves with a slow, precise stride producing the three-ring char pattern — the cool outer layer means only the footstep centre chars, not the full foot-width, and shows a complex glow: cool blue ambient outer light at 100 m, white-hot ring visible through it at 60 m, and violet-ultraviolet core flicker visible at 200 m in total darkness. Even after dispersal it can be identified from the three-ring char pattern at each step, and violet-tinted soot on passage walls from the inner core's radiation, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Ignis Elementalis Nobilis persists in deep primary vent-pressure zones of metamorphic terrain where the oldest geological fuel sources provide sustained and differentiated combustion; not confirmed in the active Gorge but inferred from the three-ring dispersal traces found at depth. It does not breed; it forms where deep primary vent-pressure in metamorphic terrain differentiates a combustion form into three distinct combustion zones, a process requiring older and more sustained fuel than the standard or major form, and recurs whenever the primary vent re-pressurises the differentiated form over a much longer interval than standard or major Ignis — weeks to months rather than hours. It sustains its mass on deep primary vent-pressure in metamorphic terrain, drawing in minimal; the nobilis form is unique among Ignis forms in that it does not consume ambient fuel at rest — it sustains through its own internal recombustion cycle, consuming only when it expands its outer layer. Range is bounded by the deep primary vent zone: outside that band the body does not collapse immediately like other Ignis forms — the three-layer architecture allows it to reduce to a cool-outer-only state (cool blue ambient glow, no internal heat perceptible) before failing; it can remain in this reduced state for days. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a vent passage in metamorphic terrain where three distinct flame-colour zones are visible at one vent mouth, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Ignis Elementalis Nobilis is roused by a threat to its vent, a significant seismic event, or another Ignis form encroaching on its primary vent zone and otherwise lies a reduced cool-blue ambient glow, producing no char-ring and no scorching — the only resting Ignis form that does not damage the floor below it. It claims a single primary vent zone and its approach passages and answers intrusion with expanding the white-hot middle layer outward before the cool outer layer — effectively reversing its normal architecture to expose the lethal middle layer to the environment — which it can hold for up to 30 minutes before the structure destabilises. Toward grey-folk it is the only Ignis form with recorded behavioural differences based on crew approach — at rest it neither tracks nor reacts to passing warmth; it responds only to direct vent-threat or another Ignis encroachment; survey crews report that Stonesong Heralds and Forgemaster senior surveyors have a shared protocol: a resting nobilis site is flagged with a violet chalk mark and left undisturbed; the three-ring char pattern in an otherwise undamaged passage is sufficient to close a lease. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Ignis Elementalis Nobilis: a body of a precisely differentiated flame humanoid with a cool blue outer layer, white-hot middle shell, and violet-ultraviolet inner core sustained by deep primary vent-pressure; immune to fire and heat in all three layers; the cool outer layer also provides a buffer against smothering attempts, since the outer layer is not consuming fuel and does not fail under smothering the way other Ignis forms do; and a defining weakness — the three-layer structure fails if the white-hot middle layer is directly targeted — specifically, a continuous water-jet sustained for over 3 minutes aimed at the middle-layer ring, which disrupts the combustion differentiation. Its senses run through the medium itself: a combination of radiant-heat sensing (standard Ignis), fuel-gradient sensing (standard), and a novel pressure-sensitivity through the primary vent that other Ignis forms lack — it detects other Ignis forms at 500 m through fuel-competition sensing. It tolerates extreme heat, darkness, and prolonged reduced-state (cool outer only) but fails at sustained water-jet targeting the white-hot middle layer. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the three-layer combustion architecture (cool blue outer, white-hot middle ring, violet core), resting non-charring, and three-ring dispersal mark, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Ignis Elementalis Nobilis are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it expands the lethal white-hot middle layer outward to expose it — 'inside-out' threat display — for up to 30 minutes; it sustains form without consuming fuel through an internal recombustion cycle, making standard fuel-starvation approaches ineffective; it detects other Ignis forms at 500 m through fuel-competition sensing and alerts them through vent-pressure modulation; and it can reduce to a cool-state (non-charring) and hold that state for days, making it effectively invisible to standard fire-hazard detection. Each is a direct expression of deep primary vent-pressure differentiating a flame body into three distinct combustion zones, so it cannot, for instance, be found in ordinary vent-fault passages, be identified by standard char-ring survey methods, or be safely extinguished by smothering. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Ignis Elementalis Nobilis matters because its three-ring dispersal trace, if found at depth in the Gorge, indicates a primary vent with geological age and structural differentiation that would redefine the Forgemasters' understanding of the lower fuel system. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as not an active field hazard in the Gorge; retained as a context entry and a comparison form for Ignis anatomy; a violet chalk close-lease marker is used wherever three-ring char is found, and its presence reshapes routes through no active route impact; a three-ring char trace in a deep survey passage triggers a violet chalk close-lease mark and Herald assessment. The Heralds' notation for a nobilis site is three concentric circles in violet chalk — the same pattern as its dispersal mark. The outer circle is drawn with a dashed line: cool, approach-safe. The middle circle solid: lethal. The inner dot: the core, which remains for weeks.
g: Ignis Elementalis Nobilis (Ignis elementalis nobilis) is the high-form fire elemental: a rare Ignis body-plan of unusual structural persistence, distinguished from the standard, minor, and major forms by a differentiated internal flame-architecture — a cool blue outer layer, a white-hot inner shell, and a violet-ultraviolet core — a heritable body-plan, not a rank. Documented from deep primary vent-pressure zones of metamorphic terrain; not confirmed in the active Gorge, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a precisely layered flame humanoid with three distinct combustion zones: cool outer, white-hot middle, and violet-ultraviolet inner core — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. Cultural traditions across the underdark describe the nobilis form as a 'prince' or 'lord' of fire, but this journal classifies it anatomically: its three-layer combustion structure and its ability to maintain form without consuming fuel are what separate it from the other three Ignis forms. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a slow extinction leaving three concentric char-rings — cool outer (1 m), white-hot middle (50 cm), and a violet-soot central mark (15 cm) and no carcass.
